user_query:
I’m preparing to brief our farm manager on soil amendments and rotation adjustments for the 2025 season. Please do the following using the files in this workspace and save all deliverables under the output/ directory.

Inputs:
- input/soil_tests.csv (latest soil lab results with last year’s crop family)
- input/rotation_plan_2025.md (planned crops and families by field for 2025)
- input/memo_template.md (memo template to be filled in)

Rules to apply:
- low_pH: flag any field with soil_pH < 6.2.
- low_N: flag any field with nitrate_ppm < 10.
- rotation_conflict: flag any field where the planned crop family (from rotation_plan_2025.md) matches the crop_family_last_year (from soil_tests.csv).

Deliverables:
1) Edit the memo template and save a new memo:
   - Read input/memo_template.md and replace only the three placeholders [TO_FILL: Soil Issues Summary], [TO_FILL: Rotation Conflicts], and [TO_FILL: Recommended Amendments] with content generated from the inputs and rules. Keep all other text unchanged.
   - Save the filled memo to output/Rotation_Advisory_Memo_2025.md.
   - Requirements for the inserted content:
     - Soil Issues Summary: include a single line with counts in the form "Counts: low_pH=X, low_N=Y, rotation_conflicts=Z" (X,Y,Z are integers), followed by two bullet lists:
       - low_pH (pH<6.2): list each field_id with its soil_pH in parentheses.
       - low_N (nitrate<10 ppm): list each field_id with its nitrate_ppm in parentheses.
     - Rotation Conflicts: list each conflicting field on its own line showing: field_id, last_year_family -> planned_family, and include last_year crop and planned crop names in parentheses.
     - Recommended Amendments: include at least one brief action line per flagged field (e.g., lime for low_pH, pre-plant N for low_N, and adjust crop choice for rotation_conflict). The exact phrasing is up to you, but ensure each field with any issue is mentioned.

2) Create a structured summary CSV of flagged fields at output/flagged_fields.csv with these columns and order:
   - field_id,soil_pH,nitrate_ppm,last_year_family,planned_family,issues
   - Include one row for every field that has at least one issue. The issues column must contain a semicolon-separated list of applicable labels in this order: low_pH;low_N;rotation_conflict (omit labels that do not apply; do not include spaces).

3) Draft an email to the farm manager and save it as output/email_to_farm_manager.txt with this structure:
   - First line: To: Rita Gomez <rita.gomez@example.com>
   - Second line: Subject: 2025 Soil Amendments and Rotation Adjustments
   - Body: a short, professional message that:
     - References the two deliverables by their exact paths: output/Rotation_Advisory_Memo_2025.md and output/flagged_fields.csv.
     - Includes three bullet lines listing the field_ids for each category:
       - Fields needing lime (pH<6.2): comma-separated list of field_ids.
       - Fields with nitrate<10 ppm: comma-separated list of field_ids.
       - Rotation conflicts (same family as last year): comma-separated list of field_ids.
     - Ends with a polite request to review.

Make sure all outputs reflect the actual data from the input files and the rules above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
